column2: What type of business does your organization operate in?
column3: How long has your business been operating?
column4: What is your role in the organization?
column5: What is the approximate size of your inventory (number of distinct products / SKUs managed)?
column6: How does your organization currently manage inventory levels?
column7: How often does your organization experience stockouts (inability to fulfill orders due to insufficient stock)?
column8: How often does your organization experience excess inventory or overstocking?
column9: On average, how many days' worth of stock does your organization hold at any given time?
column10: Which factors most significantly affect demand for your products?
column11: How predictable is the demand for your primary products on a week-to-week basis?
column12: During which period(s) does your organization typically experience peak product demand?
column13: What method does your organization currently use to forecast product demand
column14: How frequently does your organization update its demand forecasts?
column15: What is the primary source of error in your current demand forecasts?
column16: What is the average lead time from your primary supplier(s) — number of days from placing an order to receiving goods?
column17: How reliably do your suppliers meet their promised delivery timelines?
column18: Does your organization currently maintain a safety stock (buffer inventory held to guard against demand uncertainty or supplier delays)?
column19: Does your organization maintain digitally accessible historical sales data
column20: How frequently is your inventory and sales data updated in your system?
column21: Approximately what percentage of your organization's annual revenue is estimated to be lost due to inventory inefficiencies (stockouts, overstocking, or obsolete stock)?
column22: My organization's current inventory system effectively prevents stockouts and excess inventory.
column23: I trust the accuracy of our current demand forecasting method.
column24: Supplier delays are a frequent and significant source of inventory disruption in our operations.
column25: Seasonal and promotional demand fluctuations are difficult to predict accurately with our current methods.
column26: I believe predictive analytics can significantly improve the accuracy of inventory and demand decisions.
column27: My organization has sufficient data quality and availability to support the implementation of predictive analytics.
